30.5: Every word of God is tested by fire; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
30.6: Do not add anything to his words, lest he rebuke you and you be found a liar.
30.7: I ask you two things, do not refuse me before I die:
30.8: Keep falsehood and lying far from me; give me neither poverty nor riches, but grant me only the bread that is necessary for me.
30.9: lest, when I am full, I deny you and say, «Who is the Lord?» and become poor and steal and dishonor the name of my God.
30.10: Do not slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you and you bear the penalty.
30.11: There is a race that curses its father and does not bless its mother.
30.12: There is a race that is pure in its own eyes and yet is not cleansed of its defilement.
30.13: He is a race, how haughty are his gaze and how high his eyelids.
30.14: There is a race whose teeth are swords and molars knives, to devour the unfortunate from the earth and the needy among men.
30.15: The leech has two daughters: give, give. Three things are insatiable, four never say: Enough.
30.16: Sheol, the barren womb, the earth that is not satisfied with water, and the fire that never says: Enough.
30.17: The eye that mocks a father and scorns obedience to a mother, the ravens of the stream will pierce it and the young eagles will devour it.
30.18: There are three things that are beyond me, and even four that I don't understand:
30.19: the eagle's footprint in the heavens, the serpent's footprint on the rock, the ship's footprint in the middle of the sea, and the man's footprint in the young girl.
30.20: This is the way of an adulterous woman: she eats, and wiping her mouth, she says, «I have done nothing wrong.»
30.21: Under three things the earth trembles, and under four that it cannot bear:
30.22: under a slave when he comes to reign and a fool when he is full of bread,
30.23: under a scorned woman when she marries and under a servant when she inherits from her mistress.
30.24: There are four very small animals on earth, yet they are very wise:
30.25: The ants, a people without strength, prepare their food in the summer,
30.26: The hyraxes, a people without power, make their homes in the rocks,
30.27: Grasshoppers have no king and they all come out in swarms.,
30.28: You can pick up the lizard with your hand and it is located in the palace of the kings.
30.29: Three of them have a handsome appearance and four have a graceful gait:
30.30: The lion, the bravest of animals, never backing down from any adversary,
30.31: the animal with agile loins, or the goat and the king, whom no one can resist.
30.32: If you are foolish enough to let pride carry you away, and if you even have the thought of it, put your hand over your mouth,
30.33: because the pressure of milk produces butter, the pressure of the nose produces blood, and the pressure of anger produces quarrels.

31.1: Words of King Lamuel, sayings with which his mother instructed him:
31.2: What shall I say to you, my son? What shall I say to you, son of my womb? What shall I say to you, my son, object of my desires?
31.3: Do not give your strength to women, nor your ways to those who destroy kings.
31.4: It is not for kings, Lamuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, nor for the powerful to seek out fermented liquors:
31.5: lest by drinking they forget the law and distort the rights of all the unfortunate.
31.6: Give strong drink to the one who is perishing, and wine to the one whose soul is filled with bitterness:
31.7: Let him drink and forget his misery and no longer remember his sorrows.
31.8: Open your mouth in favor of the mute, for the cause of all the abandoned.
31.9: Open your mouth, pronounce just judgments, and do justice to the unfortunate and the needy.
31.10: ALEPH. Who can find a strong woman? Her price far surpasses that of pearls.
31.11: BETH. Her husband's heart trusts in her, and profits will not fail him.
31.12: GHIMEL. She does him good and not harm, every day of his life.
31.13: DALETH. She searches for wool and linen and works with her joyful hand.
31.14: HEY. She is like the merchant's ship, she brings her bread from afar.
31.15: VAV. She gets up while it is still night and provides food for her household and tasks for her servants.
31.16: ZAÏN. She thinks of a field and she acquires it; with the fruit of her hands, she plants a vineyard.
31.17: HETH. She girded her loins with strength and she fortified her arms.
31.18: TETH. She feels that her gain is good; her lamp does not turn off during the night.
31.19: YOD. She puts her hand to the distaff and her fingers grasp the spindle.
31.20: CAPH. She extends her hand to the unfortunate, she opens her hand to the destitute.
31.21: LAMED. She is not afraid of the snow for her house, because all her clothes are lined.
31.22: MEM. She makes herself blankets; byssus and purple are her clothes.
31.23: NUN. Her husband is well known at the city gates, when he sits with the elders of the land.
31.24: SAMECH. She makes shirts and sells them, and she delivers belts to the merchant.
31.25: AÏN. Strength and grace are her adornment and she laughs at the future.
31.26: PHE. She opens her mouth wisely, and good words are on her tongue.
31.27: TSADÉ. She watches over the paths of her home and she does not eat the bread of idleness.
31.28: QOPH. Her sons rise up and proclaim her blessed, her husband rises up and praises her:
31.29: RESCH. "Many girls have shown themselves to be virtuous, but you surpass them all."«
31.30: SCHIN. Grace is deceptive and beauty is vain; the woman who fears the Lord is the one who will be praised.
31.31: THAV. Give him the fruit of his hands, and let his works speak his praise at the city gates.
